questions.scenario: Users type `orders.devpolaris.com`. What is DNS responsible for in this path?

options.text: Turning the name into the next destination the browser should contact.
Proving the API has permission to read Key Vault.
Deciding the SQL transaction isolation level.

questions.explanation: DNS maps names to destinations. Identity, runtime, and database behavior are separate concerns.

questions.scenario: Why does Azure often require a TXT record when adding a custom domain?

options.text: To prove the team controls the domain before Azure accepts the hostname.
To store the TLS private key in DNS.
To make the backend health probe faster.

questions.explanation: TXT records are commonly used for ownership proof. TLS keys and backend health do not belong in public DNS records.

questions.scenario: Which evidence matters before a DNS cutover for `orders.devpolaris.com`?

options.text: The current DNS record and TTL.
Only the resource group tag on the DNS zone.

questions.explanation: A DNS cutover needs name, target, TTL, and TLS evidence. Tags help ownership but do not prove traffic behavior.
